title: Star Wars: Behind the Magic
content:
{{Top|rwm|leg|ref|italics=1}} {{VideoGame |not_appearance=1 |image=[[File:SWBehindTheMagic.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Behind the Magic'' |developer=[[LucasArts]] |publisher=[[LucasArts]] |release date=[[September 14]], [[1998]]{{WebCite|url=https://publicrecords.copyright.gov/detailed-record/voyager_11750138|text=Star Wars :behind the magic.|work=the [[Wikipedia:United States Copyright Office|U.S. Copyright Office Public Records System]]|archivedate=20251007153634}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=[[Wikipedia:Making-of|Behind the scenes]] |modes=[[Wikipedia:Single-player|Single player]] |ratings= |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer=[[Vincent Lee|Vince Lee]]''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' credits |artist=[[Mark Christiansen]] (Lead) |writer=[[W. Haden Blackman]] |composer= |timeline= |prev= |next= }} '''''Star Wars: Behind the Magic''''' is an interactive CD-ROM released in the fall of [[1998]] by [[LucasArts]]. It was designed as an in-depth look at the [[original trilogy]] and features a preview for [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. The features are spread over two discs. The very similar [[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']] was released the following year. A partial version of the software, titled ''Star Wars: Behind the Magic Vehicles Special Edition'' was released as part of ''[[The LucasArts Archives Vol. IV: Star Wars Collection II]]''.LucasArts Company Store Fall 1998 Catalog ==Details {{SectionFlag|1}}== [[File:Star_Wars_Behind_the_Magic_menu_screen.jpg|250px|thumb|''Behind the Magic''{{'s}} menu screen]] ''Behind the Magic''{{'s}} features include a detailed look at the original trilogy (with a complete script); details of the characters, locations, and technology; and a look at the making of the movies. A searchable [[Wikipedia:Glossary|glossary]] of entries is available. Several games are also included, including a trivia game and an area where you can test weapons on a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]]. The CD-ROM also included a preview of ''[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]]'', which was set to premiere in the following year. It featured a detailed look at a few elements of the film, as well as a cast list with early descriptions of the main characters. ==Continuity== [[File:Star_Wars_Behind_the_Magic_Lost_Scenes_screen.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Star Wars Lost Scenes menu screen]] Perhaps the most celebrated feature of ''Behind the Magic'' is its inclusion of several deleted scenes from [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. These include the famous "Biggs scene," where [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] talk on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and a scene where Luke talks to his friends after witnessing the opening space battle. Two other bits of rough footage are included: one of Luke watching the space battle through binoculars and a rough cut of the [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|cantina]] scene. The CD-ROM also contained a timeline of ''Star Wars'' events, which is now outdated by the release of the [[prequel trilogy]] and more [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] material. Some details later overturned include setting [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] birth year to [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]] instead of [[57 BBY/Legends|57 BBY]], placing the events of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|Episode III]] at [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], and placing the birth of the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker]] twins at [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]]. [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] is referred to as a "[[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]". The [[Prequel trilogy]] would later establish that Palpatine was never a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. The ancient history timeline says that [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] was "swayed toward the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] by the [[Force ghost|spirit]] of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]", but in fact it was the spirit of [[Freedon Nadd]] who convinced Exar Kun to use the dark side.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]][[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]][[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 4]] ==Contents - Disc 1 {{SectionFlag|1}}== ===Characters=== Most bulleted entries can be "Explore[d]", each of those offering: a "Full Description", an "Image Gallery", a "Behind the Scenes" and "BtS Images" gallery, some "Audio Clip" and a "Trivia" question. ====Main Characters====
